annotations.label: Euler's totient function

anchor.snippet: counts the positive integers up to a given integer

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `Nat.totient n` as the cardinality of naturals less than `n` coprime to `n`, with notation `φ`.

annotations.label: Totatives of 9

anchor.snippet: the totatives of

annotations.note: The set of totatives is realized as `{a ∈ range n | n.Coprime a}` but the concrete example of the totatives of 9 is not stated.

annotations.label: Multiplicativity of phi

anchor.snippet: Euler's totient function is a multiplicative function

annotations.note: `Nat.totient_mul` proves `φ(m*n) = φ m * φ n` whenever `m.Coprime n`.

annotations.label: Cototient

anchor.snippet: The cototient of

annotations.note: No definition of `n - φ(n)` as a named cototient function appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Euler's product formula

anchor.snippet: where the product is over the distinct prime numbers dividing n

annotations.note: `Nat.totient_eq_prod_factorization` expresses `φ n` as a product over prime factors `p^(k-1)(p-1)`, equivalent to the stated formula.

annotations.label: Equivalent product formulation

anchor.snippet: An equivalent formulation is

annotations.note: `Nat.totient_eq_mul_prod_factors` states `(φ n : ℚ) = n * ∏ p ∈ n.primeFactors, (1 - p⁻¹)`.

annotations.label: Phi is multiplicative

anchor.snippet: This means that if

annotations.note: `Nat.totient_mul` gives `φ(m n) = φ m * φ n` for coprime `m`, `n`.

annotations.label: Phi of a prime power

anchor.section: Value of phi for a prime power argument
anchor.snippet: If p is prime and

annotations.note: `Nat.totient_prime_pow` proves `φ(p^n) = p^(n-1)(p-1)` for prime `p` and `0 < n`.

annotations.label: phi(20) computation

anchor.snippet: the distinct prime factors of 20 are 2 and 5

annotations.note: The general product formula is available so `φ 20 = 8` is decidable, but the concrete numerical example is not stated as a lemma.

annotations.label: Totient as Fourier transform of gcd

anchor.snippet: The totient is the discrete Fourier transform of the gcd

annotations.note: No representation of the totient as a discrete Fourier transform of `gcd` was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Gauss divisor sum identity

anchor.snippet: The property established by Gauss

annotations.note: `Nat.sum_totient` proves `∑ d ∈ n.divisors, φ d = n`.

annotations.label: Möbius inversion of divisor sum

anchor.snippet: Möbius inversion applied to the divisor sum formula gives

annotations.note: Möbius inversion is available in general (`sum_eq_iff_sum_mul_moebius_eq`), but the specific consequence `φ(n) = ∑ d∣n μ(d) n/d` is not stated.

annotations.label: Möbius function

anchor.snippet: is the Möbius function , the multiplicative function defined by

annotations.note: `ArithmeticFunction.moebius` (notation `μ`) is the standard Möbius function defined via squarefreeness and the parity of the number of prime factors.

annotations.label: Upper and lower bounds for phi(n)

anchor.snippet: the top line y = n − 1 is an upper bound valid for all n other than one

annotations.note: The upper bound `φ n < n` (for `1 < n`) is `Nat.totient_lt`, but the article's lower bound `φ(n) ≥ √n` is not formalized.

annotations.label: Euler's theorem

anchor.snippet: This states that if a and n are relatively prime then

annotations.note: `Nat.ModEq.pow_totient` proves `x^(φ n) ≡ 1 [MOD n]` when `Nat.Coprime x n`.

annotations.label: Parity and divisibility of phi(n)

anchor.snippet: is even for n ≥ 3

annotations.note: `Nat.totient_even` proves `φ n` is even for `2 < n`; finer divisibility properties (e.g. `2^k | φ n`) are not isolated as lemmas.

annotations.label: Divisibility of phi(a^n - 1)

anchor.snippet: For any a > 1 and n > 6 such that

annotations.note: No statement that `n ∣ φ(a^n - 1)` (or its sharper variant) was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Menon's identity

anchor.snippet: In 1965 P. Kesava Menon proved

annotations.note: Menon's identity does not appear in Mathlib.

anchor.snippet: For every fixed positive integer

annotations.note: The asymptotic density result for `d ∣ φ(n)` is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.note: The L-series identity `∑ φ(n)/n^s = ζ(s-1)/ζ(s)` is not formalized.

annotations.note: No Lambert series representation of the totient appears in Mathlib.

annotations.note: No asymptotic `limsup` bound for `φ(n)/n^{1-δ}` is in Mathlib.

annotations.note: The combined `φ(n)·σ(n)` inequality is not formalized in Mathlib.

anchor.snippet: We also have

annotations.note: The Mertens-type `liminf` for `φ(n) log log n / n` is not formalized.

annotations.label: Nicolas inequality

anchor.snippet: The second inequality was shown by Jean-Louis Nicolas

annotations.note: Nicolas's inequality for the totient is not formalized.

anchor.snippet: For the average order, we have

annotations.note: Walfisz's asymptotic for `∑ φ(k)` is not formalized.

annotations.label: Liu's improved error term

anchor.snippet: By a combination of van der Corput's and Vinogradov's methods

annotations.note: Liu's improved error term is not formalized.

annotations.label: Coprimality probability

anchor.snippet: the probability of two randomly chosen numbers being relatively prime

annotations.note: The `6/π²` density result for coprime pairs is not formalized.

anchor.snippet: In 1950 Somayajulu proved

annotations.note: Somayajulu's `liminf`/`limsup` for `φ(n+1)/φ(n)` is not formalized.

annotations.label: Schinzel–Sierpiński density

anchor.snippet: In 1954 Schinzel and Sierpiński strengthened this

annotations.note: The Schinzel–Sierpiński density strengthening is not formalized.

annotations.label: Totient number and nontotient

anchor.snippet: A totient number is a value of Euler's totient function

annotations.note: The image/non-image set of the totient is not defined in Mathlib.

anchor.snippet: The number of totient numbers up to a given limit x is

annotations.note: Erdős's asymptotic for the count of totient numbers is not formalized.

annotations.label: Count with multiplicity

anchor.snippet: If counted accordingly to multiplicity

annotations.note: No counting-with-multiplicity asymptotic for totient values is formalized.

annotations.label: Multiplicity lower bound

anchor.snippet: It is known that the multiplicity of m exceeds

annotations.note: Multiplicity lower bounds for totient values are not formalized.

annotations.label: Ford's theorem

anchor.snippet: Ford (1999) proved that for every integer

annotations.note: Ford's theorem on totient multiplicities is not formalized.

annotations.label: Perfect totient number

anchor.snippet: A perfect totient number is an integer that is equal to the sum of its iterated totients

annotations.note: No definition of perfect totient numbers appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Gauss constructibility criterion

anchor.snippet: Gauss proves

annotations.note: Gauss's constructibility criterion via the totient is not formalized.

annotations.label: Regular n-gon construction

anchor.snippet: a regular n -gon has a straightedge-and-compass construction if n is a product of distinct Fermat primes

annotations.note: The Gauss–Wantzel theorem on regular-polygon constructibility is not formalized.

annotations.label: RSA cryptosystem

anchor.snippet: Setting up an RSA system involves choosing large prime numbers

annotations.note: RSA is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lehmer's conjecture

anchor.snippet: In 1932 D. H. Lehmer asked if there are any composite numbers

annotations.note: Lehmer's totient conjecture is not stated in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lehmer's lower bounds

anchor.snippet: In 1933 he proved that if any such

annotations.note: Lehmer's lower bounds for hypothetical counterexamples are not formalized.

annotations.label: Carmichael's conjecture

anchor.snippet: This states that there is no number

annotations.note: Carmichael's totient conjecture is not stated (Mathlib's `ArithmeticFunction.carmichael` is the unrelated reduced-totient function).

annotations.label: Riemann hypothesis equivalence

anchor.snippet: The Riemann hypothesis is true if and only if the inequality

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `RiemannHypothesis` but does not state its equivalence with a totient inequality.
